unsupported_claims.note: 'Complete Your Ritual' module with blank images is cited but NOT visible in the provided PDP screenshot (crop ends at 'Why You'll Love It'); the blank-tile claim is unverifiable from evidence. Bundle idea is reasonable but the broken-module premise is unsupported.
Favicon:false is supported by tech_signals, but the 'blank cross-sell tiles' claim cannot be verified from the provided screenshot which doesn't show the module. Half the hypothesis is ungrounded.

top_weaknesses: The 'Complete Your Ritual' broken-image claim is repeated across two experiments (exp-4b and exp-0c) but is NOT visible in the provided PDP screenshot crop, making it the report's central unverifiable assertion.
Homepage actually has benefit-segmented sections (Sleep/Immune/Energy/Digestion CTAs visible in screenshot), partially contradicting the 'generic slideshow forces self-sort' framing in exp-3c.
Some lift figures and the cross-sell module CTR KPI rest on the unverified broken-tiles premise.

Two experiments (exp-4b1c8f3e6d27 and exp-0c6b3f9a1d82) hinge on a 'Complete Your Ritual' cross-sell module with blank/broken images that is not visible in the cited PDP screenshot crop — the visual claim is unverifiable and likely overstated.
exp-5a8d2c4e9f17 speculatively links the image-format issue to the unverified blank cross-sell tiles, compounding the weak visual claim.
Reviews claim is slightly nuanced: homepage carries testimonials, so 'no social proof anywhere' is an overstatement, though PDP-level absence is real.

Two experiments (exp-4b and exp-0c) rely on a 'Complete Your Ritual' module with blank/broken images that is NOT visible in the provided PDP screenshot crop — the central visual claim is uncorroborated and possibly fabricated.
Exec summary and exp-2f imply zero social proof anywhere, but the homepage clearly displays three customer testimonials — an inaccuracy the report should have reconciled.
